answers.answer:
Helios Sentinel employs a multi-layered real-time threat detection approach that combines three complementary techniques: (1) signature-based matching for known indicators of compromise, (2) behavioral analysis for detecting deviations from established baselines, and (3) ML-driven anomaly detection for identifying novel and zero-day threats.

The platform ingests a broad range of data sources, including:
• Endpoint telemetry — process events, file system changes, and network connections
• Cloud workload logs — AWS CloudTrail, Azure Activity Log, and GCP Audit Log
• Network flow data — NetFlow v9 and IPFIX
• Email gateway events

Our SIEM correlation engine is engineered for high-throughput environments, processing up to 50,000 events per second per tenant. Average detection-to-alert latency is 2.3 seconds for signature-based matches and 18 seconds for behavioral detections, ensuring security teams receive actionable alerts in near real time.

Helios Security maintains a comprehensive portfolio of current compliance certifications, all managed under a continuous monitoring program with quarterly internal audits:

• **SOC 2 Type II** — Most recently audited December 2024 by Deloitte
• **ISO 27001:2022** — Certified March 2024 by BSI
• **FedRAMP Moderate** — Authorized June 2024 (sponsoring agency: U.S. Department of Homeland Security)
• **HIPAA** — Most recent assessment October 2024; Business Associate Agreement (BAA) available upon request
• **PCI DSS v4.0 Level 1 Service Provider** — Validated September 2024 by Coalfire
• **StateRAMP** — Authorized January 2025

In addition, Helios undergoes annual third-party penetration testing by NCC Group as part of our broader assurance program. Audit reports and certification letters can be provided under NDA upon request.
sources: Helios Data Sovereignty & Privacy Whitepaper v3.1

answers.answer:
Helios offers tiered per-seat pricing for our Endpoint Protection Platform (EPP+EDR bundle) as follows:

• 500 endpoints: $18/seat/month ($108,000/year)
• 1,000 endpoints: $15/seat/month ($180,000/year) — 17% volume discount
• 5,000 endpoints: $11/seat/month ($660,000/year) — 39% volume discount

Yes, volume discounts are built into the tiered pricing above and scale with deployment size. Additional multi-year commitment discounts are available: an extra 5% off for a 2-year term and an extra 10% off for a 3-year term.

The minimum contract term is 12 months. Optional add-ons are available at additional per-seat cost: SIEM (+$6/seat/month) and MDR (+$12/seat/month). Note that all pricing excludes professional services (e.g., onboarding, custom integrations), which would be quoted separately based on scope.
flags: Professional services pricing not included — recommend a follow-up scoping conversation if the customer requires implementation support.
Pricing sheet is dated Q1 2025; confirm it remains current at time of proposal submission.

answers.answer:
Helios Security currently serves 47 customers in the financial services vertical, broken down as follows: 12 banks, 8 insurance carriers, 15 asset management firms, and 12 fintech companies. Average NPS within this vertical is 72, reflecting strong customer satisfaction.

Three reference accounts (pre-approved for external use) are available:

1. **Meridian National Bank** — 3,200 endpoints running EPP + EDR + SIEM; customer since 2022.
2. **Crestview Capital Partners** — 850 endpoints running EPP + MDR; customer since 2023.
3. **Apex Insurance Group** — 5,100 endpoints on the full Helios platform; customer since 2021.

Formal reference calls or site visits can be arranged through your Helios account team upon request.

Helios provides full EU data residency through dedicated regional infrastructure hosted in Frankfurt (AWS eu-central-1) and Dublin (AWS eu-west-1). EU customers can select their preferred region at provisioning, and customer data never leaves the selected region — including backups, replicas, and processing workloads. A GDPR-compliant Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is included by default in all EU contracts.

Encryption at rest: All customer data is encrypted using AES-256-GCM. Customers may use Helios-managed keys via AWS KMS or supply their own keys through our Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) program for additional control over key lifecycle and revocation.

Encryption in transit: All API traffic and endpoint agent communications use TLS 1.3. Endpoint agents additionally implement certificate pinning to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks against agent-to-cloud channels.

Supporting assurance: Helios undergoes annual third-party penetration testing by NCC Group, and the platform is covered by SOC 2 Type II (Deloitte, December 2024) and ISO 27001:2022 (BSI, March 2024) certifications. Data retention is configurable per tenant, defaulting to 90 days for raw telemetry and 13 months for aggregated alerts.
sources: Helios Data Sovereignty & Privacy Whitepaper v3.1

issues: Q4 customer count math: 12 banks + 8 insurance + 15 asset management + 12 fintech = 47, which matches the stated total (consistent), but Apex Insurance Group is listed as a reference with 5,100 endpoints — this exceeds the largest pricing tier example (5,000 endpoints) in Q3, which may prompt customer questions about pricing for larger deployments.

Q3 annual pricing math at 500 endpoints: $18 × 500 × 12 = $108,000 ✓; at 1,000: $15 × 1,000 × 12 = $180,000 ✓; at 5,000: $11 × 5,000 × 12 = $660,000 ✓ — all consistent. However, the stated discount percentages (17% and 39%) are calculated against the $18/seat baseline; this is not explicitly stated and could be misread.

Tone is largely consistent and professional across answers, but Q3 is the only response with flags raised, while Q5 references penetration testing and certifications already covered in Q2 — minor redundancy rather than contradiction.

Q2 lists FedRAMP Moderate authorization (June 2024) and StateRAMP (January 2025) but Q5's EU data residency discussion does not mention any EU-specific certifications (e.g., C5, ENS, ISO 27018), which a careful reviewer might expect given the GDPR/EU emphasis.
Dates are internally consistent: SOC 2 (Dec 2024), ISO 27001 (Mar 2024), and NCC Group pen testing references match between Q2 and Q5.
recommendations: Clarify in Q3 that volume discount percentages are calculated against the 500-seat baseline rate of $18/seat/month, and consider adding a pricing tier or 'contact us' note for deployments above 5,000 endpoints (relevant given the Apex Insurance reference at 5,100 endpoints in Q4).
Consider adding EU-specific compliance attestations (e.g., C5, ISO 27018, EU Cloud CoC) to Q5 if available, to strengthen the EU data residency narrative.

Reduce minor redundancy between Q2 and Q5 by having Q5 reference Q2's certification list rather than restating SOC 2 and ISO 27001 details, or alternatively keep as-is for standalone readability — confirm reviewer preference.
Verify Q3 pricing sheet currency before submission, as already flagged in the answer itself.

architecture.approach: Sequential question processing with tool use loop

notes: ✓ All 5 questions answered with high confidence
✓ Comprehensive eval framework passes all tests
